During the takeoff roll, the aircraft experienced a <<sudden loss>> <<of>> <<engine>> <<energy>> <<due>> <<to>> <<a>> <<fuel>> <<leak>>. The pilot attempted to abort the takeoff but <<could not>> <<stop>> <<the>> <<aircraft>> <<in>> <<time>>. The plane veered off the runway, collided with a <<golf>> <<cart>> <<and>> <<a>> <<fire truck>> <<before>> <<coming>> <<to>> <<a>> <<stop>>. *highlighted section* The incident resulted in <<multiple>> <<injuries>> <<but>> <<no>> <<fatalities>>. *highlighted section*

<<The>> <<National Transportation Safety Board>> <<initiated>> <<an>> <<investigation>> <<into>> <<the>> <<incident>>. Investigators found a <<gap>> <<in>> <<the>> <<maintenance>> <<records>> <<that>> <<had>> <<not>> <<been>> <<updated>> <<for>> <<several>> <<months>>. *highlighted section* This <<gap>> <<in>> <<documentation>> <<led>> <<to>> <<a>> <<missed>> <<opportunity>> <<to>> <<detect>> <<the>> <<fuel>> <<leak>> <<before>> <<the>> <<flight>>. *highlighted section* The NTSB also noted that the pilot had <<not>> <<received>> <<sufficient>> <<training>> <<on>> <<how>> <<to>> <<handle>> <<such>> <<an>> <<emergency>> <<during>> <<takeoff>>. *highlighted section* The investigation concluded that the <<primary>> <<cause>> <<was>> <<the>> <<fuel>> <<leak>> <<combined>> <<with>> <<the>> <<lack>> <<of>> <<proper>> <<maintenance>> <<procedures>>. *highlighted section*
